title: Calycorectes latifolius
taxonomy: Calycorectes latifolius is classified in the family Myrtaceae. This family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as myrtles or bayberries. Calycorectes latifolius is native to South America, and its classification is a key part of understanding its evolution and relationships with other plants. The Myrtaceae family includes many species of trees, shrubs, and herbs that are found in tropical and subtropical regions. By classifying Calycorectes latifolius within this family, scientists can gain insights into its characteristics and behaviors.

parts: Calycorectes latifolius, also known as the "Brazilian cherry," is a species of plant that has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. intro: Calycorectes latifolius, also known as the Guatambu, is a plant species native to the Atlantic Forest of Brazil, belonging to the family Myrtaceae. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, diarrhea, and respiratory problems, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of C. latifolius include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from C. latifolius include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to support immune function and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections.
